name: procedure creating modern sanguine homunculus

phase_0.boundary_condition: Hermetic perfusion chamber and sterile containment field

VINIT.justification: The sterile, empty void and structural matrix before any biological activity or cellular seeding occurs.

TANCH.justification: The closed physical and thermodynamic boundary that contains the mature homunculus and finalizes the system.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism representing directed biological growth, mitosis, and morphogenesis toward a mature state.

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism representing programmed cell death, waste clearance, and the pruning of malformed tissue.

CLINK.justification: Composition of distinct cellular and structural layers into a unified, mechanically stable tissue architecture.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference mechanism where cells maintain their specific genomic memory and recognize their own differentiated state.

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the pluripotent cell mass into two distinct developmental paths: endothelial vascular network and parenchymal tissue core.

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the split lineages by physically and functionally fusing the vascular network with the parenchymal core to recover the whole organoid.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation confirming viable tubulogenesis, active perfusion, and healthy tissue integration.

EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation detecting oxygen deprivation, cell death, or failure of vascular connection.

ENGAGR.justification: A paradice where uncontrolled cellular proliferation and terminal differentiation are held simultaneously without resolution, shaping the organoid's complex topology.

IFIX.justification: Permanent, irreversible fixation of the homunculus's final structural and genomic state via flash-freezing and sequencing.

phase_3.void_description: Acellular hydrogel scaffold, sterile and devoid of biological activity
phase_3.true_description: Successful cellular engraftment, active perfusion, and tissue viability
phase_3.false_description: Hypoxia, necrosis, or failure of vascular anastomosis
phase_3.both_description: Teratoma formation: simultaneous uncontrolled proliferation and partial differentiation held in pathological tension

steps.domain_action: Initialize the sterile bioreactor and seed the acellular hydrogel scaffold, establishing the void state.
Load induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) with specific epigenetic memory and genomic identity.
Induce initial cellular proliferation and early morphogenesis to form a primitive cell mass.
Bifurcate the developing cell mass into two distinct lineages: the Endothelial Vascular Network and the Parenchymal Tissue Core.
Confirm successful endothelial tubulogenesis and lumen formation in the vascular T-arm.
Drive forward growth, branching, and maturation of the vascular network in the T-arm.
Detect localized hypoxia or differentiation failure within the parenchymal core in the F-arm.
Induce targeted apoptosis to clear necrotic and malformed parenchymal cells in the F-arm.
Perform microvascular anastomosis, fusing the mature vascular network and the pruned parenchymal core back into the unified Vascularized Micro-Organ Construct.
Crosslink the extracellular matrix to structurally compose and mechanically stabilize the integrated tissue layers.
Enter a chimeric state where both rapid cellular proliferation and terminal differentiation are held in tension to sculpt the homunculus's final topology.
Establish self-referential homeostatic feedback loops, allowing the tissue to autonomously regulate its own microfluidic perfusion.
Cryopreserve the mature sanguine homunculus and permanently record its genomic and structural state.
Seal the hermetic perfusion chamber, closing the system boundary and finalizing the synthetic organism.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Automated microfluidic control software translating differentiation protocols into precise valve and pump actions
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Paracrine signaling, gap junctions, and microfluidic chemical gradients
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Epigenetic methylation patterns and extracellular matrix structural memory
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Circadian biomimetic lighting and automated perfusion pulse timers
phase_5.alfs_store: The reference human genome and standardized iPSC biobank

phase_6.cycle_cost: Nutrient-rich synthetic blood, oxygen, and ATP consumed per perfusion cycle
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Undifferentiated or partially differentiated cellular aggregate
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Highly structured, vascularized, and metabolically active micro-organ
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The mature homunculus reaches a homeostatic steady state where metabolic entropy production is perfectly balanced by the bioreactor's continuous waste clearance and nutrient input.
